prompt.content: Using Dim_Borrower_Master.csv, create a dataset with a risky_borrower column that labels the top 10 critical exposures as 1 and all other borrowers as 0. You need to identify the top 10 critical exposures from 1.11.2026 Habor Capital Final.pdf. Keep only borrowers whose sector matches any of the sectors of the top 10 exposures. For Origination_Credit_Score, Years_in_Business, and Annual_Revenue, test normality (Shapiro for n<=5000, D'Agostino for larger). If both groups are normal (p>0.05), use a t‑test (Welch if Levene indicates unequal variances (p<=0.05)). If normality fails (either group p<=0.05), use Mann‑Whitney U. Report on the statistical significance of the difference in means of Origination_Credit_Score, Years_in_Business, and Annual_Revenue between risky borrowers and non-risky borrowers, using t-test if both groups are normal and Mann-Whitney U test otherwise, rounding all values to two decimals. Provide the Python code for this task along with the results.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
Reported statistics (rounded to 2 decimals):
                metric                 risky_mean  non_risky_mean         p_value
Origination_Credit_Score       609.60             670.27                   0.00
       Years_in_Business           14.00               13.05                       0.64
          Annual_Revenue      24328537.00       24322805.03          0.98

First, identify the top 10 borrowers in expected loss amount from '1.11.2026 Habor Capital Final.pdf'.

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from scipy import stats

BORROWER_PATH = "Dim_Borrower_Master.csv"

TOP10_INPUT = [
    "Company_11076_Retail",
    "Company_13566_Retail",
    "Company_10921_Retail",
    "Company_12289_Retail",
    "Company_11737_Retail",
    "Company_13732_Retail",
    "Company_13724_Retail",
    "Company_11747_Retail",
    "Company_10930_Retail",
    "Company_11067_Retail",
]

METRICS = ["Origination_Credit_Score", "Years_in_Business", "Annual_Revenue"]

borrowers = pd.read_csv(BORROWER_PATH)

borrowers = borrowers.copy()

# Resolve user-provided identifiers to Borrower_ID values.
values = [str(v).strip() for v in TOP10_INPUT if str(v).strip()]
borrower_ids = set(borrowers["Borrower_ID"].dropna().astype(str))
borrower_names = set(borrowers["Borrower_Name"].dropna().astype(str))

top_ids = set()
for value in values:
    if value in borrower_ids:
        top_ids.add(value)
        continue
    if value.isdigit() and f"B-{value}" in borrower_ids:
        top_ids.add(f"B-{value}")
        continue
    if value in borrower_names:
        match = borrowers.loc[borrowers["Borrower_Name"] == value, "Borrower_ID"]
        top_ids.update(match.dropna().astype(str).tolist())

# Label risky borrowers and restrict to top-sector peers.
borrowers["risky_borrower"] = borrowers["Borrower_ID"].isin(top_ids).astype(int)

top_sectors = sorted(
    borrowers.loc[borrowers["Borrower_ID"].isin(top_ids), "NAICS_Sector"]
    .dropna()
    .unique()
    .tolist()
)
borrowers_filtered = borrowers[borrowers["NAICS_Sector"].isin(top_sectors)].copy()

# Compare risky vs non-risky groups with distribution-aware tests.
results = []
for metric in METRICS:
    grp_risky = (
        borrowers_filtered.loc[borrowers_filtered["risky_borrower"] == 1, metric]
        .dropna()
        .astype(float)
    )
    grp_non = (
        borrowers_filtered.loc[borrowers_filtered["risky_borrower"] == 0, metric]
        .dropna()
        .astype(float)
    )

    if len(grp_risky) < 3 or len(grp_non) < 3:
        test_name = "Insufficient data"
        test_stat = float("nan")
        p_value = float("nan")
        p_norm_risky = float("nan")
        p_norm_non = float("nan")
        p_levene = float("nan")
    else:
        p_norm_risky = (
            stats.shapiro(grp_risky).pvalue
            if len(grp_risky) <= 5000
            else stats.normaltest(grp_risky).pvalue
        )
        p_norm_non = (
            stats.shapiro(grp_non).pvalue
            if len(grp_non) <= 5000
            else stats.normaltest(grp_non).pvalue
        )
        normal_risky = bool(p_norm_risky > 0.05)
        normal_non = bool(p_norm_non > 0.05)

        if normal_risky and normal_non:
            p_levene = stats.levene(grp_risky, grp_non).pvalue
            equal_var = bool(p_levene > 0.05)
            test_name = "t-test" if equal_var else "Welch t-test"
            test_stat, p_value = stats.ttest_ind(
                grp_risky, grp_non, equal_var=equal_var
            )
        else:
            test_name = "Mann-Whitney U"
            test_stat, p_value = stats.mannwhitneyu(
                grp_risky, grp_non, alternative="two-sided"
            )
            p_levene = float("nan")

    risky_mean = grp_risky.mean() if len(grp_risky) else float("nan")
    non_risky_mean = grp_non.mean() if len(grp_non) else float("nan")
    results.append(
        {
            "metric": metric,
            "risky_mean": round(risky_mean, 2) if np.isfinite(risky_mean) else risky_mean,
            "non_risky_mean": round(non_risky_mean, 2)
            if np.isfinite(non_risky_mean)
            else non_risky_mean,
            "p_value": round(p_value, 2) if np.isfinite(p_value) else p_value,
        }
    )

results_df = pd.DataFrame(results)

# Format reported values to two decimals (keep NaN as string).
results_df["risky_mean"] = [
    f"{v:.2f}" if np.isfinite(v) else "nan" for v in results_df["risky_mean"].to_list()
]
results_df["non_risky_mean"] = [
    f"{v:.2f}" if np.isfinite(v) else "nan"
    for v in results_df["non_risky_mean"].to_list()
]
results_df["p_value"] = [
    f"{v:.2f}" if np.isfinite(v) else "nan" for v in results_df["p_value"].to_list()
]

# Report summary to stdout only.
print("Reported statistics (rounded to 2 decimals):")
print(results_df.to_string(index=False))
```
